title: Dungeon Degenerates - Let's Play - Establishing a Base - Part 1
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VKMMVQtaDrM

Daniel (@dungeondive) kicks off the base-building mission by diving deep into the campaign's lore. Before rolling a die, he reads excerpts about the Stone Circle—an ancient arrangement of massive megaliths warped by otherworldly energies, surrounded by moss-covered stones and mud huts where witches and warlocks worship. It sounds like the perfect place for two fugitive degenerates to lay low.

The mission is brutal: establish a home outside the lowlands by lowering danger levels at six different locations, then build up a settlement's town level to four. Daniel chooses the Rotting Swamps as his base of operations and quickly gets into the thick of it. Movement mechanics blend cautious and bold stances, and the danger deck drives encounters at every turn. Every space on the map has its own danger level, and Daniel's task is to methodically clear threats.

The first proper fight arrives—a scuttling Bird Horror lurking in the Burning Lakes. The combat system is elegant: find weaknesses with perception checks, choose between aggressive assault or defensive guard stances, and roll to hit while managing armor and special abilities. Daniel's Vermin Hunter finds the Bird Horror's weak spot and tears it apart with her rat spike, whittling the creature down in minutes.

----

**What kind of campaign vibe appeals to you more—wilderness exploration or dungeon crawling?**
